title: veronica austriaca subvar. tenuis
intro: Veronica austriaca subvar. tenuis, also known as Austrian speedwell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the plantain family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Veronica austriaca subvar. tenuis include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often combined with other herbs to create remedies for colds, flu, and other respiratory ailments.
